title: The Imaginary Road Trip to Nana's House
summary: Peacock the Wizard leads a colorful group of friends on a grand quest to visit Nana in the world of imagination. Piling into a purple and pink car with blue stripes, the team travels past veggie stores and taco shops, enjoying snacks like chocolate chip and fried veggie tacos. Their journey ends at Nana’s cozy house, where they reunite with family and pets before drifting off to sleep after a reading of their favorite bedtime story. In a smaller tale, Oscar Mouse finds the perfect tiny home inside a sugar can in a dark cupboard.

highlightQuote.text: I want chocolate chips with fried veggies inside tacos, tacos.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Stars and stories. Do you want to sing it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Stars and stories, stars and stories, stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I like looking at the stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I want you to tell a story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, what should we tell a story about? Um, Tinkering?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Or about LEDs?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: LEDs? I don't like LEDs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, should we tell a story about Peacock the Wizard?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. Yes, and all of his friends.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: His Sesame Street friends. Big Bird and everyone else.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Even Big Bird and even little bird and my stuffie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Big bird and Little Bird? Okay. Once upon a time, everyone was over at Peacock the Wizard's castle having a picnic.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, they were inside playing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They were inside the castle playing. And Peacock the Wizard said, "I have a quest for all of you. If you can find three things that all start with the letter P, I will give to you a magical treasure. Can you do this for me?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, right now, Peacock the Wizard, I can't do it for you because right now I'm thinking about my Nana.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see, kiddo, said Peacock the Wizard. What are you thinking about her?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because I miss my Nana and I need Nana to buy more mice for me and more bunnies and more tables and more chairs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see, said Peacock the Wizard. That's why I can't go on the quest. I see, said Peacock the Wizard. Perhaps we can go on a different quest to visit Nana in our imaginations. Would you like to do that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would! And does Nana have more tables and more chairs and more bunnies and mice?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I believe that she does, but we will have to find out when we visit her. Now then, to go on this new quest to visit Nana in our imaginations, first we must decide how we will get to Nana's house. How will we travel there, kiddo?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hmm, first take a left and then go to the right. That's the way you get to Nana's, and then you have to go to the taco place and the veggie store place, with the dried fruit, even the dried raspberries.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. It sounds like a long journey. Will we take a helicopter or a boat or a car?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We will take a car.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what color car shall we drive?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A purple and pink car.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A purple and pink car. Well, I happen to have a purple and pink car—

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: With blue stripes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: With blue stripes. I happen to have a purple and pink car and I'll call Mistress Mouse up and see if she can paint the stripes on. Just a moment. Oh yes, she agrees. She'll do that right away. And meanwhile, let's pack some suitcases. What shall we pack for our journey?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Clothing and stuff like that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's pack some clothing. And maybe some snacks?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, snacks are a good idea because I already have my fruit but I'm still a little bit hungry.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, and some toys too. And maybe some books. Hmm, what book would you want to take with you to Nana's?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I would like my favorite book.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What's your favorite book?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The "Goodnight, Goodnight, Construction Site".

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's take that one. And now it's time, since we've packed everything up and Mistress Mouse has painted the blue stripes on the purple and pink car for us to get in. "I'll drive," said Peacock the Wizard, "and you can get in the back, Izzy the kiddo. Is anyone else coming with us?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, all the friends.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All the friends. Okay, everyone get in the car. And Big Bird and Little Bird and Cookie Monster and Bert and Ernie and Oscar the Grouch and Grover... everybody got in the car. And now the car was very full and Peacock the Wizard said, "Okay everyone, buckle up, it's time to go!" and he started the car, vroom! And they drove down the road, away from the Wizard's castle and onto the highway and they went fast, vroom vroom vroom vroom vroom. And they drove for a long time. And what did they do while they were driving?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were listening to stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what story did they listen to?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ours with Bert and Ernie watching stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: By telling stories and their imagination.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then Cookie turned off the stars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, he did? Why'd he do that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because he wanted to play Legos and he didn't see what he was going to play.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. And so in this way they passed the time in the car as they drive along the highway. And now it's time to take a stop. We're halfway to Nana's. Where are we going to stop?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First, some veggies.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We stop for some veggies? That sounds like an excellent place to stop. Okay, everybody get out and stretch your arms and stretch your legs a little bit. It's been a long drive. Okay, we'll pick up some veggies and anything else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, dried raspberries.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What'd you say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dried raspberries.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dried raspberries also. Yes, very good. Okay, we'll get those things. Oh, now it's time to get back in the car and drive. Vroom vroom vroom vroom vroom vroom.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The next stop is taco place.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The next stop is taco place. Oh, we're here already actually. Here's the taco place. Everyone out! Okay, let's get some tacos. What kind of taco do you want, Izzy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want chocolate chips with fried veggies inside tacos, tacos.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, a taco with chocolate chips and fried veggies. Perfect. And I'll have maybe the pork belly taco, I like that one. And some chips and guacamole. Okay, well that was a good stop everyone.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Num num.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Let's get back in the car and we'll drive a long way further on the highway, vroom vroom vroom vroom vroom. And then we're slowing down, we turn off the highway, we'll drive down the road, and then oh look, there's Nana's driveway. We turn onto Nana's driveway, we drive the car through the trees and up the hill and down the hill and then peeking between the trees is a beautiful house with a chimney making smoke. And there's Nana's house. And so we pull up in front and we get out of the car and we open the door and who do we see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nana, Grandpa Rick, and Drew.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They're all there, your family.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Even Grandpa Rick, no even Garfield and Ginger.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, they're all here. And they're so happy to see you. They say, "Welcome Izzy, we love you, we missed you so much!" And then it's starting to get dark, so we have a little dinner and then we get ready for bed and we read one story with Nana. What do we read?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The "Goodnight, Goodnight, Construction Site".

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We read our one book and then we sing our lullaby. "Isabella kiddo, it's time to sleep." And then it's time for bed. And so we all go to sleep at Nana's house. And that's the end of our story for the night.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do we have time for one more story?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we could tell one more very short story. What should we tell a short story about? Maybe Oscar Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. A short story about Oscar Mouse. Once upon a time, Oscar Mouse was living in the attic and he wished for a different place to go and call his own. And so he searched all around the house until he finally found a dark cupboard.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and inside the cupboard what did he find? Nothing. Just a tiny bit of a sugar can, and he curled up inside some newspaper and he fell fast asleep in it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And that's the end. That's our short Oscar story. Okay, and now it's time to turn the lights on and turn off the stars.

characters.name: Peacock the Wizard
characters.description: Once a deep-ocean companion to Seeker, this vibrant bird wizard now serves as the charismatic host of the magical castle’s grand show. From a cozy nest atop a soaring tower, they orchestrate wonder through balloon school arts—transmuting rubber into succulent strawberries—while proudly sporting a magnificent hat gifted by Hattie and Izzy. A playful visionary of dreamer magic and a tireless seeker of rare diamond-shaped potions, they use their stage to beckon heroes into a world of soaring imagination, often dispatching them on whimsical quests to recover enchanted new hats for their growing collection.

characters.name: Big Bird
characters.description: A large yellow bird friend who joins the journey to Nana's house.

characters.name: Little Bird
characters.description: A diminutive, felt-feathered avian from the world of Sesame Street who wedges himself into the car for every adventure, sustaining his whimsical frame on a breakfast of exactly one grain of salt.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Cookie Monster
characters.description: This azure beast of Sesame Street, driven by an eternal craving for cookies and perpetually swaddled in a plush blanket peppered with illustrations of his favorite treats, inadvertently snuffed out the constellations with a clumsy flick of a switch during a moonlit drive to a Lego assembly.

characters.name: Bert
characters.description: Stepping from the quiet ink of a library book to join the trek into the deep dark, this classic figure from a shared basement apartment maintains the rhythmic precision and meditative focus of a sleep-story weaver. Formerly the host of grand pigeon galas who folded his roommate Ernie’s vibrant chaos into serene harmony, he now applies a caretaker’s grace to the encroaching shadows. Whether smoothing mud-stained hems into crisp geometries or sorting through the abyss’s chaotic clutter, he treats the exploration of the unknown as a soothing ritual—a singular, exacting discipline of folding the world’s wilder reaches back into a state of restorative order.

characters.name: Ernie
characters.description: Bert’s classic companion and roommate moves through their shared world with the jittery stealth of a mouse, their impatient haste betraying them with a trail of unintended squeaks as they assist in the search for the lost pearl. Often found in a futile pursuit of order—whether frantically sweeping thick mud or overwhelmed by a cascade of forgotten toys—this iconic figure remains a soul perpetually caught between domestic chaos and a sudden drenching from a garden hose. Yet, on the long journey for the treasure, their nervous energy settles into a rhythmic wonder; they trade the mess of the earth for the fixed, silent patterns of the cosmos, weaving evocative stories of the stars to guide their path.

characters.name: Oscar the Grouch
characters.description: A green friend who joins the group for the long journey.

characters.name: Grover
characters.description: A furry blue monster who is part of the crew traveling to Nana's.

characters.name: Grandpa Rick
characters.description: A family member who greets the group upon their arrival at Nana's house.

characters.name: Drew
characters.description: A person present at Nana's house to welcome the travelers.

characters.name: Garfield
characters.description: Minnie and Mickey’s intrepid kitten is a seasoned morning commuter, navigating the school run to reach Seeker’s sanctuary for heavy napping and premium kibble. A social fixture known for sharing twilight dinners with Ginger or waiting expectantly at Nana’s for a warm welcome, they occasionally trade their public life for the quiet solitude of a closet, sequestering themselves away with a treasured hoard of toys.

characters.name: Ginger
characters.description: A soft, round, and silver-coated feline, this exceptionally fluffy educator is one of Minnie and Mickey’s own kittens grown into a figure of quiet authority. Known for daintily distributing napkins with velvet paws and commuting to school behind the wheel, the gray-furred teacher balances his role on Nana’s welcoming committee with a life of polite, helpful service. Whether presiding over his humble sanctuary—the very cage Seeker once coveted—or sustaining himself on crunchy kibble and savory wet food, he remains a steady presence, most recently seen trading his classroom duties for the shadows of a secret closet hideout alongside Garfield.

characters.name: Oscar Mouse
characters.description: Roused by the spring warmth of a forested green planet, this tiny mouse artisan dwells within a small house of weathered stone. Having migrated from a drafty attic to the crystalline sanctuary of a sugar can, he works deep within his dark, stony cupboard, meticulously mending every dent with the smooth application of squeak oil.

places.name: Peacock the Wizard's Castle
places.description: Perched atop a verdant hill, this grand castle serves as a magical sanctuary and the storied venue for the realm’s most spectacular shows. Beyond its formidable gate, the warmth of the kitchen and the echoing splendor of the feast hall provide a staging ground for imaginative quests, while a winding spiral staircase beckons the brave upward toward the spotlight of the wonders that await.

places.name: Veggie Store
places.description: A stop on the highway where the group buys snacks like dried raspberries.

places.name: Taco Place
places.description: A restaurant known for its unique tacos filled with chocolate chips and fried vegetables.

places.name: Nana's House
places.description: A beautiful house with a smoking chimney marks the end of a long, wooded driveway, serving as the crew’s secondary destination as they navigate the vastness of the stars.

places.name: The Attic
places.description: At the spaceship’s highest point—a vital hub for testing critical systems and the original home of Oscar Mouse before his move—lies a dusty alcove within Isabella’s home; there, in a vault Seeker once discovered, a fractured crown still glimmers with a stubborn, broken brilliance.

places.name: Dark Cupboard
places.description: The location where Oscar Mouse finds a sugar can to use as a bed.
